themes: Cursor -23% gross margin & model dependency risk
OpenClaude open-source response to Claude Code pricing

worthClicking.author: @ClaudeDevs
worthClicking.why: Official thread on Claude Code's web/mobile refresh — teleport flag for web-to-terminal handoff and speed/reliability rebuild are product details worth tracking.

worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: Brockman's framing of OpenAI's launch week as a coherent 'new way to work with computers' is the strategic narrative competitors need to understand.

worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: GPT-5.5 topping CursorBench is a concrete benchmark claim that directly affects model-selection decisions for AI product teams.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Cursor's -23% gross margin at $2.7B ARR is the clearest data point yet on how dangerous model-layer dependency is for AI product distribution plays.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: OpenClaude v0.6 dropping free/open-source precisely as Anthropic pushed Claude Code to $100+ is the pricing-gap story that could reshape the indie dev tooling market.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick calling agent org design and multi-agent benchmarking 'the next critical frontier' is a useful framing for where AI product strategy gaps actually live.

worthClicking.author: @levie
worthClicking.why: Levie's Jevons paradox argument — AI productivity gains increase demand for labor rather than replacing it — is the most important counternarrative to AI-kills-jobs thinking for enterprise sales.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Anthropic's own experiment showing Opus-powered agents extract 70% more revenue than Haiku-powered ones on identical tasks is a direct sales enablement data point.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: The Claude Routine pulling calendar + email context before every meeting is a concrete agentic workflow pattern worth stealing for enterprise demos.

voices.why: Primary source for Anthropic product updates; teleport/routines/web-CLI handoff details are directly relevant to AI product strategy.
Brockman's launch-week framing posts are the canonical OpenAI strategic narrative, not hype — worth reading first.
Consistently applies economic frameworks (Jevons, enterprise adoption) to AI with enough specificity to be useful for sales strategy.
Agent org design and benchmarking gaps are underserved topics he surfaces before most; low volume, high signal.

examples: aakashgupta newsletter plugs and YouTube stubs repeated ~15 times
RoundtableSpace all-caps content-farm hype posts with minimal original analysis
signulll philosophy/vibe posts and DataChaz paid Hypescribe promotion threads

emerging.theme: Cursor -23% gross margin & model dependency risk
